route.name: San Diego High Country Overnighter
route.description: Public GPX Southern California high-country loop with technical singletrack, dirt roads, pavement, and campground logistics.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Southern California high-country loop with technical singletrack, dirt roads, pavement, and campground logistics.
route.bestSeason: Spring and fall are ideal; July can be blisteringly hot even at altitude.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Southern California high-country loop with technical singletrack, dirt roads, pavement, and campground logistics.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Food and water around Laguna Mountain, Cuyamaca, and Descanso; carry 2-3 liters minimum.
resources.food: Laguna Mountain, Cuyamaca, and Descanso services; confirm hours.
resources.camping: Developed campgrounds and some dispersed options exist; reservations/rules must be verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Reject for core July weekend unless a cool forecast appears.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Current July 10-13 screening blocks gate confirmation. Keep as planning/reference only.
San Diego High Country Overnighter is not gate-confirmed until July heat, campground reservation, fire closure, and technical-rider fit are all verified.
Source-season guidance and subagent triage flag summer as potentially blisteringly hot even at altitude.
July heat gate blocked by route's own summer warning plus no verified NWS point forecast for July 10-13.
Fire risk gate blocked by active San Diego County CAL FIRE incident and Southern California seasonal drying outlook.
AQI/smoke gate not cleared for ride dates.
Route-specific closure overlay against Cleveland NF and State Park trail/road closures not completed.
Cuyamaca availability confirms only the campground gate; it does not clear July heat, Range Fire/fire-weather, AQI/smoke, trail/road closure, or water gates.
requiredFixes: Resolve the July-window blocker or choose a different route.
Set a heat cutoff and verify forecast/AQI/fire closures.
Recalculate water capacity for July heat.
Confirm the rider accepts technical singletrack and Noble Canyon descent exposure.
Recheck NWS point forecast no earlier than July 7 and reject if excessive heat, Red Flag Warning, Fire Weather Watch, thunderstorms, or unsafe overnight lows appear.
Verify Range Fire status and any evacuation/closure overlap before promotion.
Confirm exact legal overnight site and water availability/hours for the chosen itinerary.
Check AirNow/fire.airnow.gov on July 9 and ride mornings; require AQI/smoke acceptable for sustained exertion.
Pick a specific Paso Picacho or Green Valley site/cabin on ReserveCalifornia and record the booking or site-level hold before any promotion.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Two-day duration fits, but only if heat and fire checks pass and the intended rider is comfortable with technical Noble Canyon-style riding.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Cuyamaca Rancho State Park's official page showed reservation availability YES for two-consecutive-night starts on July 10, 11, 12, and 13, 2026, but a specific Paso Picacho or Green Valley site/cabin still has to be selected and held.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Needs last-week fire closure, heat advisory, smoke, and forecast check before it can be reconsidered.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Technical singletrack and park/forest corridors need route-level legal/open-status confirmation.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Marked water exists in route notes, but July capacity and longest gaps must be recalculated.
promotionChecklist.mapArtifactsNote: After `python3 -m bikepacking_planner build-trip route-library`, exported GPX and GeoJSON exist and route is present in manifest.

days.camp: Developed campgrounds and some dispersed options exist; reservations/rules must be verified.

waypoints.name: Dispersed camping off Pine Creek Road
Beautiful views out over the Anza Borrego Desert

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Subagent GPX/artifact check: public GPX available from Bikepacking.com.
Subagent water note: food/water are marked and normally 2-3L capacity may suffice depending on temperature, but July heat changes that assumption.
Camp availability worker found Cuyamaca Rancho official availability YES for July 10, 11, 12, and 13, 2026 two-night starts, subject to site-level booking.

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent reported the route has a public GPX and plausible developed camp/cabin options, but summer heat and technical difficulty prevent gate confirmation without a fresh forecast and reservation.
Wild camping is not permitted in key park/meadow areas; a developed campground or cabin must be confirmed.
Exported GPX exists after rebuild with 19158 GPX points; GeoJSON and manifest entry also exist.
Public route located; 66 miles over 2 days in Cuyamaca/Laguna, with route-source warning that summers can be blisteringly hot even at altitude.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: BIKEPACKING.com San Diego High Country Overnighter

readinessEvidence.finding: Current restrictions checked July 2; reservation availability shows YES for July 10, 11, 12, and 13, but exact legal overnight and water availability still need confirmation.
CAL FIRE active incidents page lists Range Fire in San Diego County, started 2026-06-30, 429 acres, 80% containment; Southern California outlook is warmer/drier than normal.

Cuyamaca Rancho State Park's official page showed reservation availability YES for two-consecutive-night starts on July 10, 11, 12, and 13, 2026; the same page identifies Paso Picacho and Green Valley as reservation-system campgrounds.
